sourceEvidence.label: Subtrochanteric Fracture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Subtrochanteric Fracture

payload.oneLiner: Subtrochanteric fractures are high-stress proximal femoral shaft injuries where reduction and implant choice determine alignment survival.
pimpQuestions: Why is varus a major concern?
When is an atypical pattern suspected?
orSurvivalTips: Describe reduction tools and nail starting point language
Mention sciatic nerve protection with posterior exposure
caseSteps: Localize fracture relative to lesser trochanter

sourceEvidence.label: Subtrochanteric Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Subtrochanteric Fracture

objectives: Localize subtrochanteric fractures relative to the lesser trochanter.
Explain high mechanical demands and varus shortening forces.
Recognize atypical fracture morphology and sciatic nerve safety in fixation.

metadata.description: Subtrochanteric fracture classification by fracture line and comminution.
Proximal femoral diaphyseal fracture distal to the lesser trochanter with high mechanical demands.

relationship_metadata.note: subtrochanteric region defined distal to lesser trochanter

metadata.claim_text: Cephalomedullary nailing is the common operative answer for most subtrochanteric fractures on board-style questions.
Missing atypical subtrochanteric morphology in a low-energy patient on long-term bisphosphonates.
Describe fracture level relative to lesser trochanter, comminution, implant plan, reduction strategy, and weight-bearing restrictions.
Subtrochanteric fractures occur in a high-stress diaphyseal transition zone with significant varus and shortening forces.

metadata.action: Proceed with cephalomedullary nail fixation with attending-confirmed reduction and implant strategy
metadata.trigger: Unstable subtrochanteric fracture pattern requiring intramedullary stabilization

metadata.claim_text: Varus malunion after subtrochanteric fracture alters abductor tension and gait mechanics.

metadata.action: Discuss extended workup, implant choice, and contralateral surveillance with attending before standard fragility pathway
metadata.trigger: Atypical subtrochanteric morphology in low-energy patient with bisphosphonate or metabolic bone disease concern

metadata.description: Plate fixation option for selected subtrochanteric fracture patterns.
Transverse/beaking pattern suggesting bisphosphonate-related or low-energy atypical morphology.
Varus malalignment after subtrochanteric fracture with gait and abductor consequences.

metadata.claim_text: Posterior proximal femur exposure for plate fixation places the sciatic nerve at risk and requires deliberate protection.
